title: scutellaria bolanderi subsp. typica
intro: Scutellaria bolanderi subsp. typica, also known as Bolander's skullcap, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the western coast of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as baicalein and baicalin. The health benefits of Bolander's skullcap include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Bolander's skullcap can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or supplement, and is often used in herbal medicine to support mental health and overall well-being.
